item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
consider me impressed. audio was epic. nice to see a new unique interpretation of the foundation.

really good use of Wikidot to improve the article.

mad this hasn't gotten MORE ATTENTION.

+1
Whoa, what happened. Thanks for your time and I'm glad that you enjoyed the read!

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
MEMES!

Cause we all know we need em.

Made by YossiPossi
– hide block

item.title: Re: +50!
item.content:encoded: As mentioned above, we are glad that you enjoyed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm actually quite glad you made the password easy to spot (I saw "ROSES" with zero prompting), because I'm super dumb about finding stuff like that, ignoring the fact that I also missed where the password actually goes. :B

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah apologies about that. I'm assuming your talking about the first 2 collapsibles, which in retrospect, are quite easy to miss. We'll make some amendments to make the collapsible easier to find. We didn't want to make password to hard, as we felt doing so would ruin the mood of the story, but like you've said, we haven't quite found the right balance yet.

Thank you for your feedback, we'll be sure to use it to improve the story!
Honestly never expected it to come this far! Thanks for all your support!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd complain about how easy it is to find the password, except that I actually totally didn't notice the collapsible that held the first part. In fact, that was a big issue: some of the collapsibles were hard to find, and others it seems like were clickable but did nothing? Is something not working right with the theme? I could have done without it, is the point, it actually made the article harder to read.

Still, I like that this is anomalous malware designed to exploit a vulnerability instead of "mai 133t haXX0Ring skillz defeet Fundashun ololol!!1!1"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
^ This. The Kafkaesque nightmare of Foundation command isn't explored enough until the password section, in which we're told of all this. It leaves the thematic pacing pretty damn backloaded, which diminishes the impact of the first half of the piece.

Furthermore, there's some prose issues throughout, especially in the first half of the piece. Your best foot forward should be just that: best.

item.title: Re: Marvellous coding
item.content:encoded: They used listpages so you'll want to check the fragments to find it. (i.e. fragment:500contestjak…)

item.title: Marvellous coding
item.content:encoded: How did you make collapsibles work inside collapsibles? (how could you hide the source code…) I click for the "Page Source" button and only saw a short CSS code block and a Listpage module.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my God - All I could say at the end.
Easy +1 for me, reading it I really felt like a symathetic, curious Argost so by the end I felt really sad :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a very good point! Apologizes, I'm not directly familiar with C. Will implement a hotfix on that though. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this, +1 for me

However, in most coding languages, especially those in the C family and PHP include comments either with //insert comment or /* insert comment here */, with the former being used only for one line comments. So if /* is used, it needs to be closed, or else everything under will be considered as a comment as well.

But good job! Fits the theme well, fairly interesting, well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much! We wanted to make this character "Director D. Bold" to have motives as well as become apart of something greater. I personally had plans of starting a GoI with Bold (I guess now known as Broken Rose) and continue from the premise we staged from this story.

Your opinion is appreciated! Thank you for your kind words

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it! The log at the end is what makes it for me. I love pieces that question the very reality and motives of the foundation, and you hit the nail on the head here by making that the end of the mystery. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm happy to know you enjoyed it! Doctor Fullham spent a lot of time doing the voice recording, which really helped with this article, so due credit to him!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! We have made the appropriate changes, you're opinion is very valid. However, unfortunately we cannot change the audio or monologue of the second part. Not only would Doctor Fullham have to record everything over again, changing the monologue now would detract from that theme of perfectionism. The monologue we have already was actually decided after many discussions with critters, and it was the best we could come up with that sounded reasonable. But thank you! Your concerns about the MCL have been resolved, at the very least. :)

item.title: Re: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
I highly appreciate your opinion. More than anything, we just want to make this as best as possible. I have went forward with adding some changes, considering that this hasnt been the first time I've seen someone get curious by that Interview Log.

Unfortunately the monologue and aspects of the second part cannot be reverted. Not only because the voice acting has already finalized (and I dont want to put Fullham in that situation again), but also because changing it would detract from the theme we were trying to go for. We aimed to target "perfectionism" and with talks with many critters, the monologue as we had it was the best we could make it without sacrificing the emotion and/or theme.

That being said, I have made the appropriate changes. Thank you, your opinion is appreciated

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
NoVote for now - the concept is amazing but certain parts of the execution detract from it. Perhaps make the mechanism by which it opens the locks a bit less of an asspull (either make it more mundane/purely electronic or elaborate on how it was determined to be "interdimensional) and also clean up the final monologue to be a bit less Frank Castle-y - maybe look through the Scarlet King 001 proposal for inspiration.
You guys have struck gold with the idea, though! With a bit of work you can make it perfect!

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
[+ Opinion (spoilers?)]
– hide block

After reading this, I was left disappointed for a number of reasons. Firstly, the first iteration of the article goes on far too long, and I almost lost interest before actually typing in the password. Things like the log with Captain Druid honestly just came across as attempts to put in self-inserts more than anything else.

As for the passcode, it was far too easy to find. The notes left nothing up in the air and told me specifically where to look and what for. Even without them, clues two and three would have been far too obvious and I wouldn't ever have found clue 1 with my lowered phone background. Essentially, that aspect of the article didn't improve my experience and only added extraneous information IMO.

In the final iteration, everything up to the final log was either uninteresting exposition or overly creepypasta-esque junk code with cliché spooky messages. The final log is nothing special either. It's a run-of-the-mill dude trying to sabotage the Foundation because of their morally questionable actions. It's been done a lot, and the monologue was too reminiscent of comic book superheroes to interest me.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was so happy to have been even a small part of this wonderful work. Jak and Druid both busted their butts on writing a compelling mystery and pulling off some SWEET code to make this all look dope as hell. The audio files were something I randomly decided to record and I was flattered when they wanted to include them in the article. I hope you all enjoy my contribution to this fantastic submission but Jak and Druid really deserve all the credit!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was kinda suspicious at first, but after entering the password it all became clear and… damn it was a good read.
(Also: top quality voice acting!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad to have been one of the people that saw this before publishing. Well done Jak, best of luck too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! The first line you presented was actually supposed to be a red herring to kind of ignore Bolds presence in the log. Perhaps if I changed that line it would be better?

The second line was moreso to explain the logic of dimensional replacement.

My theme was with perfectionism and the idea of people abusing that. Your opinion is appreciated!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think my reaction to this was most obvious on reading the following:

It was later concluded that the 5 individuals who were discovered near Site 119 were likely associated with the Chaos Insurgency.

This has been concluded to be a side effect of the magnet being substituted and replaced through several dimensions of space-time.

These two statements, at the end of their respective logs, tell me information that was not communicated through the observed logs themselves, without any evidence or justification, and seem to negate the purpose of the preceding logs at least to some extent. I enjoyed working out the password, and this is nicely presented, but too much of the writing felt as though it didn't contribute to the story and theme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We appreciate that! Thank you for your comment. It helps a lot to know that at least someone enjoys it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
holy crap this is really good

+1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
When you don't care about the little people…

This collab wouldn't have been possible without JakdragonX inviting me to join him on this ride. This was massive fun to write, and God if Doctor Fullham didn't scare the shit out of me with his voice recording.

Once again, massive thanks to everyone who contributed to this article, be it critting, reading or simply mucking about with us in 19! Happy Reading folks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ItsDenali Thank you so so much! As long as people enjoy it that's all I really want :) IronDruid really did the work though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't even begin to imagine the sheer amount of time and dedication you poured into this article, but rest assured it payed off more than you know. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! It was a lot of time and effort so I'm really glad you enjoyed it! Thank you for letting us know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is probably the most beautiful SCiP I’ve ever seen. I’m also very glad you gave away the password for simple folk like me.

+1, and this is my new favourite so far.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This was a collab from myself and IronDruid! (Also, if anyone asks, yes the first clue is kinda wonky. Black Highlighter currently has a bug where it doesn't give a background to false blackboxed text.)

PASSWORD
YOU LAZY :P

BROKEN ROSES FALL

Land of the Free-To-Use Images
Ahh

Photos under the "Image Analysis" tab

Link: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1510519138101-570d1dca3d66?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=crop&w=1031&q=80

Photo by Nikita Kachanovsky on Unsplash

All photos published on Unsplash can be used for free. You can use them for commercial and noncommercial purposes. You do not need to ask permission from or provide cC10606it to the photographer or Unsplash, although it is appreciated when possible.

SCP Logo

Link: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/SCP_Foundation_%28emblem%29.svg/800px-SCP_Foundation_%28emblem%29.svg.png
Photo Provided by Wikipedia

At the author's website is the following stated: "Unless otherwise stated, the content of this page is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 3.0 License." No author has been mentioned but will give cC10606it if notified.

Cred's to Critters
Thank Y'all Very Much

Doctor Fullham —> Clinical Tone + Logical Flaws + VOICED DIRECTOR BOLD
Kyza —> General Overview/Thoughts
ManyMeats —> Pointed out several flaws with Conprocs and Description.
Simartar —> General Overview/Thoughts x2
Francis —> General Thoughts and Help!
Cerastes —> General Overview/Thoughts
Popsioak —> Assisted With Clues
Tracque_ —> Helped with coding the password!
ihp —> Helped with coding the listpages!
stormbreath —> Helped with general thoughts and code!
AFrivoulousPursuit —> General Overview/Thoughts
RockTeethMothEyes —> Assisted With Clues

+ Everyone else!!!!

Here are our 5 choices for if we can't win!
SCP-5999
SCP-5500
SCP-5555
SCP-5001
SCP-5050

(Intro to a new GoI… ¯\_(ツ)_/¯)
